content: I always thought this life is a videogame. It turned out to be theatre.
One day I woke up and it was 8 am.
Is there anything else we truly own except the dreams and hopes?
Maybe discipline is just remembering why you started.
The older I get, the more I realize that most people are just trying to figure things out while pretending they already know.
Some days you don't need motivation. You just need to begin.
I spent years trying to find the perfect path. Turns out paths are created by walking.
Interesting how a place can hold so many memories even after everyone leaves.
The hardest part about learning something new is accepting that you will look stupid before you become good.
Coffee tastes different when you have something you are building.
I think we underestimate how much our environment shapes us. Change the room, change the people, change the inputs — and suddenly you become someone else.
A lot of anxiety disappears when you start doing the thing you were avoiding.
The internet gave everyone a voice. Now the hard part is saying something worth hearing.
Building something from nothing is a strange feeling. Nobody cares at first, but you still wake up thinking about it.
Maybe success is not becoming someone else. Maybe it is finally becoming yourself.
There is something beautiful about being a beginner.
The most dangerous person is not the one who knows everything. It's the one who is curious enough to keep learning.
I used to think confidence came before action. It actually comes after you survive doing hard things.
A random thought: every person you pass today has an entire universe happening inside their head.
The weird thing about time is that you never notice the last time something happens until it has already become a memory.
